project.name: ruvector slice 8 — Robotics
project.description: Slice 8 of RuVector covering robotics: the agentic-robotics-* crates (core pub/sub, real-time executor, MCP, Node.js bindings, embedded, benchmarks) plus the ruvector-robotics platform (perception, cognitive layers, behavior trees, swarm intelligence, and ruvector vector-DB bridge).

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the agentic-robotics-benchmarks crate, defining three Criterion benchmark targets (pubsub_latency, message_passing, latency) that profile robotics middleware throughput.

nodes.name: pubsub_latency.rs
nodes.summary: Criterion benchmark suite measuring Zenoh/RustDDS pub-sub latency including publisher/subscriber creation, single-publish latency, throughput at varied batch sizes, end-to-end latency, CDR vs JSON serializer comparison, and concurrent publisher scaling.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses criterion's bench_with_input and sample_size tuning to characterize tail latency under load.

nodes.name: benchmark_publisher_creation
nodes.summary: Measures the cost of constructing a Publisher<RobotState> over a Zenoh topic, isolating allocator and middleware-handshake overhead.

nodes.name: benchmark_subscriber_creation
nodes.summary: Benchmarks Subscriber<RobotState> construction latency to assess DDS discovery and queue allocation costs.

nodes.name: benchmark_publish_latency
nodes.summary: Measures single-message publish latency on a steady-state Publisher, reflecting per-call CDR serialization plus transport hand-off cost.

nodes.name: benchmark_publish_throughput
nodes.summary: Throughput benchmark publishing 10/100/1000 message batches per iteration to characterize bulk send performance scaling.

nodes.name: benchmark_end_to_end_latency
nodes.summary: End-to-end latency benchmark using a manual iter_custom timer to measure publish-to-receive round-trip through the wire.

nodes.name: benchmark_serializer_comparison
nodes.summary: A/B benchmark comparing CDR (ROS2-compatible) and JSON serializer formats on identical RobotState payloads.

nodes.name: benchmark_concurrent_publishers
nodes.summary: Scales 1/2/4/8 concurrent publishers each on distinct topics to expose contention and per-topic overhead under multi-talker robot scenarios.

nodes.name: message_passing.rs
nodes.summary: Criterion micro-benchmarks for the core robotics message layer: async publish via tokio runtime, plus CDR vs rkyv zero-copy serialization throughput.

nodes.name: message.rs
nodes.summary: Core message type system for the agentic-robotics middleware: the Message trait plus ROS2-compatible payload structs (RobotState, PointCloud, Pose, Point3D) used across publisher/subscriber pipelines.

nodes.name: Message
nodes.summary: Marker trait identifying types that can be serialized and shipped over the robotics middleware, exposing type_name() and version() for wire compatibility.

nodes.name: RobotState
nodes.summary: Canonical robot pose+velocity+timestamp payload type used in benchmarks and example pipelines; the headline 'hello-world' message for the platform.

nodes.name: PointCloud
nodes.summary: Sensor-aligned point cloud message carrying Point3D samples with per-point intensities and capture timestamp; consumed by perception pipelines.

nodes.name: Pose
nodes.summary: 6-DOF pose message pairing a position vector with an orientation quaternion for transform and navigation traffic.

nodes.name: serialization.rs
nodes.summary: Serialization façade supporting CDR (ROS2 wire format), rkyv zero-copy (stubbed), and JSON encoders, wrapped behind a Serializer enum dispatcher used across publishers and subscribers.

nodes.name: Format
nodes.summary: Enum tagging which wire format (Cdr, Rkyv, Json) a Serializer should apply, exposed to user code via publisher constructors.

nodes.name: Serializer
nodes.summary: Dispatcher wrapping a Format choice and routing serialize() calls to the matching CDR/JSON/rkyv backend; defaults to CDR for ROS2 compatibility.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the embedded MCU runtime crate, gating Embassy async executor and hardware peripherals behind no_std features for cross-compilation to robot controllers.
nodes.languageNotes: Feature-gated dependencies typical for no_std embedded crates supporting multiple MCU targets.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the MCP (Model Context Protocol) bridge crate that exposes agentic-robotics tools to LLM agents, with optional features for transport variants and dev-only test deps.

nodes.name: server.rs
nodes.summary: Minimal MCP server scaffolding: a fluent ServerBuilder plus helpers (tool registration, text_response, error_response) that translate robotics primitives into MCP-spec JSON responses for LLM agent consumption.

nodes.name: ServerBuilder
nodes.summary: Fluent builder constructing an McpServer with a name and version before assembling the tool registry exposed to AI agents.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Node.js/TypeScript user guide for the NAPI-RS robotics bindings, with quick-start, autonomous navigator and multi-robot examples, full Publisher/Subscriber API reference, performance numbers, and ROS2 compatibility notes.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: NAPI-RS binding layer exposing AgenticNode/AgenticPublisher/AgenticSubscriber to Node.js, marshalling JSON between V8 and the Rust pub-sub core with async publish, try_recv, and recv primitives.
nodes.languageNotes: NAPI-RS pattern: Arc<RwLock<HashMap>> registries plus napi::Result<T> async functions surfaced as JS Promises.

nodes.name: AgenticNode
nodes.summary: Node.js-facing robotics node managing a registry of publishers and subscribers under a named identity, mirroring the ROS2 rclpy Node API surface.

nodes.name: AgenticPublisher
nodes.summary: NAPI publisher handle taking a JSON-encoded string from JS, parsing into a Value, and forwarding to the inner Zenoh Publisher with stats reporting.

nodes.name: AgenticSubscriber
nodes.summary: NAPI subscriber handle supporting non-blocking try_recv() and async recv() that returns Optional<string> JSON payloads to JS callers.

nodes.name: latency.rs
nodes.summary: Criterion micro-benchmarks for the real-time runtime: latency-tracker record overhead and high-priority executor spawn latency.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Real-time runtime crate root re-exporting executor/latency/scheduler submodules and defining the RTPriority enum (Background through Critical) used to dispatch control-loop tasks under deadline.

nodes.name: RTPriority
nodes.summary: Real-time priority ladder (Background, Low, Normal, High, Critical) with From<u8>/From<RTPriority> conversions used by the executor to map onto OS scheduling classes.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Architecture overview for the ruvector-robotics crate explaining its bridge/perception/cognitive/mcp module split, quick-start usage, design philosophy, and the sub-100us perception latency targets.

nodes.name: cognitive_loop.rs
nodes.summary: End-to-end example exercising the perceive→think→act→learn loop of CognitiveCore against synthetic sensor inputs to demonstrate the full autonomous cognition cycle.

nodes.name: swarm_coordination.rs
nodes.summary: Multi-robot swarm example registering robots with a SwarmCoordinator, assigning tasks, computing formation positions, and running a consensus proposal across the fleet.

nodes.name: gaussian.rs
nodes.summary: Converts dense robot point clouds into compact GaussianSplat clouds for 3D viewer rendering, computing per-cluster centroids, scales, and opacity from variance statistics with trajectory annotations.
nodes.languageNotes: Variance-driven scale computation with floor clamp prevents degenerate gaussians on collinear clusters.

nodes.name: GaussianSplat
nodes.summary: Single 3D gaussian primitive (center, color, opacity, scale, label, trajectory) consumed by the WebGL viewer to render perceived objects.

nodes.name: GaussianSplatCloud
nodes.summary: Frame-tagged collection of GaussianSplats representing one perception tick's visualisable scene state.

nodes.name: GaussianConfig
nodes.summary: Tunables for the splat conversion (cell_size, min_cluster_size, default_color, base_opacity) controlling visual density and clutter rejection.

nodes.name: gaussians_from_cloud
nodes.summary: Top-level entry point clustering a PointCloud and mapping each surviving cluster into a GaussianSplat via cluster_to_gaussian.

nodes.name: cluster_to_gaussian
nodes.summary: Reduces a cluster of Point3D samples to a single GaussianSplat by computing the centroid, per-axis sqrt-variance scale, and density-scaled opacity.

nodes.name: to_viewer_json
nodes.summary: Serialises a GaussianSplatCloud into the WebGL viewer's expected JSON shape with trajectory expansion (collapsing to a single-position list when no trajectory is present).

nodes.name: pipeline.rs
nodes.summary: Per-frame perception pipeline that rebuilds a spatial index from each SensorFrame, detects radial obstacles around the robot, and predicts short-horizon trajectories from a rolling position history.

nodes.name: PipelineConfig
nodes.summary: Tunables for the perception pipeline including obstacle_radius search bound and a track_trajectories flag that turns off prediction for sensor-only mode.

nodes.name: PerceptionResult
nodes.summary: Per-frame output of the perception pipeline carrying detected obstacles, an optional Trajectory prediction, and microsecond frame latency.

nodes.name: PipelineStats
nodes.summary: Rolling pipeline telemetry counters (frames_processed, avg_latency_us, objects_tracked) used to expose health metrics to dashboards.

nodes.name: PerceptionPipeline
nodes.summary: Stateful pipeline owning a SpatialIndex, position history buffer, and obstacle counter; orchestrates per-frame ingest, neighbour query, and trajectory smoothing.

nodes.name: cognitive_core.rs
nodes.summary: Implements the cognitive perceive→think→act→learn loop with attention-thresholded percept admission, confidence-ranked decision selection, emergency-mode preemption, and reward-driven threshold adaptation.
nodes.languageNotes: Rust VecDeque used as bounded percept buffer; emergency CognitiveMode short-circuits deliberation.

nodes.name: CognitiveMode
nodes.summary: Top-level cognitive operating mode (Reactive, Deliberative, Emergency) that gates how aggressively decisions are taken.

nodes.name: CognitiveState
nodes.summary: Fine-grained lifecycle state (Idle, Perceiving, Thinking, Acting, Learning) exposing where the cognitive loop currently is for instrumentation.

nodes.name: ActionType
nodes.summary: Closed set of robot action verbs (Move, Rotate, Grasp, Release, Speak, Wait) emitted by the decision engine.

nodes.name: CognitiveCore
nodes.summary: Main cognitive engine holding the percept buffer, decision history and cumulative reward; implements perceive/think/act/learn used by examples and the MCP executor.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Module barrel for the cognitive subsystem, re-exporting cognitive_core, world_model, behavior_tree, decision_engine, memory_system, skill_learning, and swarm_intelligence.

nodes.name: world_model.rs
nodes.summary: World model maintaining a registry of TrackedObjects with kinematic prediction plus a 2D occupancy grid with Bresenham-like path-clearance queries used by the planner.

nodes.name: TrackedObject
nodes.summary: Object the robot is tracking over time with id, position, velocity, last-seen timestamp, confidence, and human-readable label.

nodes.name: WorldModel
nodes.summary: Stateful world model coupling tracked-object HashMap with a 2D occupancy grid, supporting stale-object eviction, linear-extrapolation state prediction with confidence decay, and grid-walk path-clearance checks.

nodes.name: executor.rs
nodes.summary: MCP tool dispatcher routing LLM-agent JSON requests to perception pipeline methods (detect_obstacles, build_scene_graph, predict_trajectory, focus_attention, detect_anomalies, spatial_search, insert_points) with per-call latency tracking.
nodes.languageNotes: Argument parsing helpers (parse_point_cloud, parse_position, parse_json_arg) tolerate both raw JSON values and stringified JSON to handle different MCP client encodings.

nodes.name: ToolExecutor
nodes.summary: Owns a PerceptionPipeline and SpatialIndex and dispatches MCP ToolRequests onto seven handler methods, returning ToolResponses with elapsed-microsecond timings.

nodes.name: config.rs
nodes.summary: Perception subsystem configuration with serde roundtrip-tested SceneGraphConfig, ObstacleConfig, and the composite PerceptionConfig used by the scene graph builder and obstacle detector.

nodes.name: SceneGraphConfig
nodes.summary: Scene-graph tuning parameters including cluster_radius, min_cluster_size, max_objects cap, and edge_distance_threshold for proximity-based edge creation.

nodes.name: ObstacleConfig
nodes.summary: Obstacle-detection thresholds (min_obstacle_size, max_detection_range, safety_margin) tightening or loosening sensitivity to clutter.

nodes.name: PerceptionConfig
nodes.summary: Composite perception configuration that bundles SceneGraphConfig and ObstacleConfig for the pipeline to consume in one shot.

nodes.name: scene_graph.rs
nodes.summary: Builds proximity-based scene graphs from raw point clouds or pre-clustered objects, computes pairwise edges with semantic 'near'/'far' relations, and merges scenes across frames with timestamp-preserving deduplication.

nodes.name: PointCloudSceneGraphBuilder
nodes.summary: Builder that turns clustered point clouds into a SceneGraph with bounded object counts, computes 3D-distance edges, and supports multi-frame merging.

nodes.name: rvf.rs
nodes.summary: RVF (RuVector File) packing layer that adapts robotics primitives (point clouds, scene objects, trajectories, gaussian splats, obstacles) into the RuVector vector store with strict dimension enforcement and kNN query passthrough.
nodes.languageNotes: Each pack_* method asserts dimension at the boundary via check_dim() to fail fast before touching RvfStore.

nodes.name: RvfPackError
nodes.summary: Error enum for the packing layer covering underlying RvfStore errors, EmptyData, DimensionMismatch, and IO failures with a From<RvfError> conversion.

nodes.name: RoboticsRvf
nodes.summary: Robotics-aware wrapper around RvfStore exposing pack_point_cloud, pack_scene_objects, pack_scene_graph, pack_trajectory, pack_gaussians, and pack_obstacles plus a dimension-checked query_nearest for kNN retrieval.

nodes.name: executor_performance.rs
nodes.summary: Criterion benchmark suite measuring ROS3Executor creation, task spawning, scheduler overhead, async execution, priority handling, and deadline distribution.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Criterion benchmark groups with custom Deadline/Priority types from the agentic-robotics-rt crate.

nodes.name: benchmark_executor_creation
nodes.summary: Benchmarks ROS3Executor instantiation cost via Criterion bench_function with black_box guards.

nodes.name: benchmark_task_spawning
nodes.summary: Measures throughput of spawning real-time tasks at microsecond and millisecond deadline scales.

nodes.name: benchmark_scheduler_overhead
nodes.summary: Quantifies PriorityScheduler enqueue/dequeue overhead under varied workloads.

nodes.name: benchmark_task_distribution
nodes.summary: Benchmarks task distribution across worker threads in the ROS3 executor.

nodes.name: benchmark_async_task_execution
nodes.summary: Measures async task execution latency through the executor's tokio runtimes.

nodes.name: benchmark_priority_handling
nodes.summary: Evaluates how the scheduler reorders tasks with mixed priority levels.

nodes.name: benchmark_deadline_distribution
nodes.summary: Benchmarks scheduling efficiency across a spread of deadline values.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for agentic-robotics-core declaring Zenoh, rustdds, tokio and serialization dependencies plus a message_passing benchmark.

nodes.name: error.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the core Error enum used across the agentic-robotics-core crate via thiserror.

nodes.name: middleware.rs
nodes.summary: Wraps Zenoh as the primary pub/sub middleware with a small configuration struct used to open sessions.

nodes.name: Zenoh
nodes.summary: Wrapper struct that opens and exposes a Zenoh session for pub/sub messaging.

nodes.name: ZenohConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration struct holding mode and listener endpoints for opening a Zenoh session.

nodes.name: service.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the Queryable and Service abstractions plus ServiceStats for tracking robotics service request/response traffic.

nodes.name: Queryable
nodes.summary: Queryable endpoint abstraction with a handler and registered statistics.

nodes.name: ServiceStats
nodes.summary: Tracks request, success, and error counters for a robotics service.

nodes.name: Service
nodes.summary: Service abstraction for invoking robotics endpoints with name and stats accessors.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Overview of agentic-robotics-embedded as a no-std crate with RTIC and Embassy support for sub-50KB embedded robotics targets.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive guide to controlling robots via the Model Context Protocol, with quickstarts, tool registration patterns, and integration examples for Claude and other AI assistants.

nodes.name: transport.rs
nodes.summary: Implements StdioTransport, a stdin/stdout line-delimited JSON transport for MCP protocol traffic between AI clients and the robotics server.

nodes.name: StdioTransport
nodes.summary: MCP transport that reads JSON-RPC requests from stdin and writes responses to stdout via a registered handler.

nodes.name: build.rs
nodes.summary: Minimal Cargo build script for agentic-robotics-node (currently a no-op placeholder).

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the agentic-robotics-rt real-time runtime, depending on the core crate plus tokio/rayon and exposing a latency benchmark.

nodes.name: executor.rs
nodes.summary: Implements ROS3Executor with separate high/low priority tokio runtimes plus blocking thread pool, modeling Priority and Deadline types for real-time robotics tasks.

nodes.name: Priority
nodes.summary: Enumeration of task priorities used to route work to high or low priority runtimes.

nodes.name: Deadline
nodes.summary: Wrapper around a Duration carrying the deadline for a real-time task spawn.

nodes.name: ROS3Executor
nodes.summary: Real-time executor coordinating high/low priority tokio runtimes and a blocking pool, exposing spawn_rt, spawn_high, spawn_low, and spawn_blocking entry points.

nodes.name: scheduler.rs
nodes.summary: Implements PriorityScheduler, a deadline-aware min-heap scheduler over ScheduledTask entries used by the ROS3 executor.

nodes.name: ScheduledTask
nodes.summary: Heap entry pairing a task identifier with its priority and deadline for ordering.

nodes.name: PriorityScheduler
nodes.summary: Min-heap based priority scheduler supporting schedule, next_task, pending_tasks, and clear operations.

nodes.name: robotics_benchmarks.rs
nodes.summary: Massive Criterion benchmark battery covering point cloud conversion, spatial search, obstacle detection, trajectory prediction, attention, behavior trees, scene graphs, memory recall, full pipelines, swarm assignment, gaussian splatting, planners, sensor fusion, and MCP tool execution.
nodes.languageNotes: Single benchmark file containing 19 self-contained Criterion suites with synthetic data generators.

nodes.name: bench_point_cloud_conversion
nodes.summary: Benchmarks conversion between sensor point cloud formats and the internal vector representation.

nodes.name: bench_spatial_search
nodes.summary: Benchmarks nearest-neighbor and radius queries against the SpatialIndex.

nodes.name: bench_obstacle_detection
nodes.summary: Measures latency of the perception pipeline's obstacle detection over synthetic point clouds.

nodes.name: bench_trajectory_prediction
nodes.summary: Benchmarks trajectory prediction for moving agents over multiple horizon lengths.

nodes.name: bench_attention_computation
nodes.summary: Measures attention focus computation over candidate scene objects.

nodes.name: bench_behavior_tree_tick
nodes.summary: Benchmarks behavior tree tick throughput under varying node counts.

nodes.name: bench_scene_graph_construction
nodes.summary: Measures scene graph build latency from detected objects of varying scene density.

nodes.name: bench_memory_recall
nodes.summary: Benchmarks episodic and semantic memory recall under increasing memory sizes.

nodes.name: bench_full_pipeline
nodes.summary: End-to-end pipeline benchmark from sensor input through perception, cognition, and action selection.

nodes.name: bench_swarm_task_assignment
nodes.summary: Benchmarks multi-robot task assignment using cost-matrix and auction-style allocations.

nodes.name: bench_gaussian_splatting
nodes.summary: Benchmarks rendering throughput of 3D gaussian splatting on robot scene reconstructions.

nodes.name: bench_astar_planning
nodes.summary: Measures A* path planning latency over grid maps of varying size.

nodes.name: bench_potential_field_planning
nodes.summary: Benchmarks potential field motion planning under varying obstacle counts.

nodes.name: bench_sensor_fusion
nodes.summary: Benchmarks multi-sensor point cloud fusion and voxel downsampling stages.

nodes.name: bench_mcp_tool_execution
nodes.summary: Measures latency of MCP tool dispatch through the robotics tool registry.

nodes.name: obstacle_detection.rs
nodes.summary: Standalone example that feeds a synthetic point cloud through the perception pipeline and prints detected obstacles.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Builds a synthetic point cloud, runs perception, and reports detected obstacle clusters with statistics.

nodes.name: config.rs
nodes.summary: Defines BridgeConfig and DistanceMetric for the robotics-to-vector bridge, controlling dimensionality and indexing parameters.

nodes.name: DistanceMetric
nodes.summary: Enumerates Euclidean, Manhattan, and Cosine distance metrics selectable for spatial indexing.

nodes.name: BridgeConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration for the perception-to-vector bridge specifying dimensionality, metric, and indexing capacity.

nodes.name: indexing.rs
nodes.summary: Implements SpatialIndex with KNN and radius search over point clouds using selectable Euclidean, Manhattan, or cosine metrics.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses BinaryHeap with MaxDistEntry for KNN; manually implemented metric distance functions.

nodes.name: MaxDistEntry
nodes.summary: Ordered heap entry pairing a candidate point with its computed distance for top-K selection.

nodes.name: IndexError
nodes.summary: Error enum surfaced by indexing operations such as dimension mismatch or empty index queries.

nodes.name: SpatialIndex
nodes.summary: In-memory spatial index supporting insert_point_cloud, KNN search_nearest, and search_radius queries with pluggable distance metrics.

nodes.name: search.rs
nodes.summary: Defines result and alert types (Neighbor, SearchResult, ObstacleAlert, AlertSeverity) returned by the robotics search bridge.

nodes.name: AlertSeverity
nodes.summary: Severity levels (Info, Warning, Critical) assigned to obstacle alerts.

nodes.name: Neighbor
nodes.summary: Represents a nearest-neighbor result with index, distance, and optional metadata.

nodes.name: SearchResult
nodes.summary: Container holding the list of neighbors and metadata for a spatial search response.

nodes.name: ObstacleAlert
nodes.summary: Alert payload describing a detected obstacle with location, severity, and optional descriptor.

nodes.name: decision_engine.rs
nodes.summary: Utility-based DecisionEngine that scores ActionOption candidates against weighted criteria and picks the best.

nodes.name: ActionOption
nodes.summary: Candidate action with associated cost and benefit features for utility evaluation.

nodes.name: DecisionConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration describing utility weights used when evaluating action options.

nodes.name: DecisionEngine
nodes.summary: Engine that computes utility values for candidate actions and returns the highest-scoring option.

nodes.name: skill_learning.rs
nodes.summary: SkillLibrary that learns skills from demonstrations and exposes execute and improve operations across stored skills.

nodes.name: Demonstration
nodes.summary: Captures a sequence of state-action pairs comprising an expert demonstration.

nodes.name: Skill
nodes.summary: Represents a learned skill with policy parameters, success rate, and metadata.

nodes.name: SkillLibrary
nodes.summary: Stores learned skills, ingests demonstrations, and exposes execute/improve/get/len operations.

nodes.name: domain_expansion.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the RoboticsDomain task generator covering clustering, avoidance, scene graph, skill sequencing, and swarm formation tasks with scoring, embedding, and reference solutions for evaluation harnesses.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes ~21 free/method functions and a 6-variant RoboticsCategory enum driving task generation.

nodes.name: RoboticsCategory
nodes.summary: Enumerates robotics task categories such as clustering, avoidance, scene graph, skill sequencing, and swarm formation.

nodes.name: TaskObstacle
nodes.summary: Obstacle descriptor used in generated avoidance tasks (position and radius).

nodes.name: TaskSkill
nodes.summary: Skill descriptor used in generated skill-sequencing tasks (name, preconditions, effects).

nodes.name: RoboticsTaskSpec
nodes.summary: Specification of a generated robotics task carrying category-specific parameters such as points, obstacles, skills, and goals.

nodes.name: RoboticsSolution
nodes.summary: Solution structure returned by candidate solvers including clusters, paths, sequences, and formations.

nodes.name: RoboticsDomain
nodes.summary: Domain implementation generating clustering/avoidance/scene-graph/skill-sequencing/swarm tasks with corresponding scoring, embedding, and reference solutions for evaluation.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Robotics MCP module defining tool parameters, schemas, the RoboticsToolRegistry, and a register_defaults populating ~30 robotics tools for AI assistant orchestration.

nodes.name: ParamType
nodes.summary: Enumerates JSON-schema parameter types (string, number, boolean, array, object) used by MCP tool definitions.

nodes.name: ToolParameter
nodes.summary: Describes a single MCP tool parameter (name, type, description, required flag).

nodes.name: ToolCategory
nodes.summary: Groups robotics tools into categories such as perception, navigation, manipulation, and communication.

nodes.name: ToolDefinition
nodes.summary: Complete MCP tool definition (name, description, category, parameters) plus to_schema converter to JSON-schema.

nodes.name: ToolRequest
nodes.summary: Incoming MCP tool invocation carrying the tool name and JSON arguments.

nodes.name: ToolResponse
nodes.summary: MCP tool response with ok/err constructors and serializable result payload.

nodes.name: RoboticsToolRegistry
nodes.summary: Central registry exposing register_tool, list_tools, get_tool, list_by_category, to_mcp_schema, and register_defaults to seed the built-in robotics tool catalog.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Defines PerceptionPipeline orchestrating obstacle detection, scene graph construction, trajectory prediction, attention focusing, and anomaly detection, exposing supporting SceneGraphBuilder and Anomaly types.

nodes.name: PerceptionError
nodes.summary: Error variants produced by perception stages such as empty input or invalid thresholds.

nodes.name: Anomaly
nodes.summary: Anomaly record produced by the perception pipeline (location, severity, optional descriptor).

nodes.name: SceneGraphBuilder
nodes.summary: Builds scene graphs from raw point clouds or detected obstacles and supports merging incremental updates.

nodes.name: PerceptionPipeline
nodes.summary: Top-level perception pipeline coordinating obstacle detection, scene graph building, trajectory prediction, attention, and anomaly detection with configurable thresholds and frame counters.

nodes.name: sensor_fusion.rs
nodes.summary: Implements multi-sensor point cloud fusion with voxel downsampling and a configurable FusionConfig.

nodes.name: FusionConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration controlling voxel size, max points, and fusion thresholds for sensor point cloud combination.

nodes.name: integration.rs
nodes.summary: Integration test suite exercising bridge type roundtrips, spatial indexing, perception pipeline, cognitive loop, behavior tree, memory recall, skill learning, swarm assignment, world model tracking, and the MCP registry.

nodes.name: test_bridge_types_roundtrip
nodes.summary: Verifies that bridge data types serialize and deserialize without loss.

nodes.name: test_spatial_index_insert_search
nodes.summary: Exercises SpatialIndex insertion plus KNN and radius search across the configured metrics.

nodes.name: test_perception_pipeline_end_to_end
nodes.summary: Runs a full perception pipeline pass over a synthetic point cloud and asserts obstacle detection results.

nodes.name: test_cognitive_loop
nodes.summary: Validates the cognitive perception-decision-action loop on a multi-action scenario.

nodes.name: test_behavior_tree_sequence
nodes.summary: Asserts behavior tree sequence node correctly chains child success/failure outcomes.

nodes.name: test_memory_store_recall
nodes.summary: Verifies that episodes stored in memory can be recalled with semantic similarity queries.

nodes.name: test_skill_learning_cycle
nodes.summary: Tests the SkillLibrary cycle from demonstration ingestion to execution and improvement.

nodes.name: test_swarm_task_assignment
nodes.summary: Checks that multi-robot task assignment respects cost minimization and unique allocation.

nodes.name: test_world_model_tracking
nodes.summary: Exercises world model object tracking across multiple frames with position updates.

nodes.name: test_mcp_registry
nodes.summary: Validates RoboticsToolRegistry default registrations, lookup by name, and category listing.

nodes.name: message_serialization.rs
nodes.summary: Criterion benchmarks comparing CDR vs JSON serialization throughput and message-size scaling for the agentic-robotics message system.
nodes.languageNotes: Rust criterion bench harness; declares four benchmark functions invoked by criterion_group!/criterion_main!.

nodes.name: benchmark_cdr_serialization
nodes.summary: Criterion benchmark measuring CDR serialization throughput across multiple message types and payload sizes.

nodes.name: benchmark_cdr_deserialization
nodes.summary: Criterion benchmark measuring CDR deserialization performance for round-trip messages.

nodes.name: benchmark_json_vs_cdr
nodes.summary: Head-to-head comparison benchmark between JSON and CDR serialization formats for equivalent payloads.

nodes.name: benchmark_message_sizes
nodes.summary: Sweeps message sizes to characterize serialization performance across small, medium, and large payloads.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive 41-section guide positioning agentic-robotics-core as a ROS2-compatible pub/sub messaging core with performance comparisons, API reference, and migration guidance.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Crate root for agentic-robotics-core re-exporting publisher, subscriber, message, middleware, serialization, and service modules and providing an init() bootstrap.
nodes.languageNotes: Rust crate root that aggregates pub/sub primitives via pub use re-exports.

nodes.name: init
nodes.summary: Initializes the agentic-robotics-core runtime, setting up logging and global messaging state.

nodes.name: publisher.rs
nodes.summary: Typed Publisher abstraction that serializes messages onto a topic and tracks per-publisher statistics like bytes and message counts.

nodes.name: Publisher
nodes.summary: Generic typed Publisher that serializes messages of type T to a named topic with a pluggable serializer and live stats counter.

nodes.name: PublisherStats
nodes.summary: Lightweight stats struct counting messages_sent and bytes_sent for a Publisher instance.

nodes.name: subscriber.rs
nodes.summary: Subscriber abstraction supporting blocking, non-blocking, and async receive over a topic-bound channel, with clone semantics for fan-out consumption.

nodes.name: Subscriber
nodes.summary: Topic-bound message receiver wrapping a channel with sync, try, and async recv methods plus clone support.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Crate root for the embedded variant exposing EmbeddedPriority levels and an EmbeddedConfig with tick rate and stack size knobs for resource-constrained targets.

nodes.name: EmbeddedConfig
nodes.summary: Runtime configuration struct for embedded deployments specifying tick_rate_hz and stack_size with a default impl.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Model Context Protocol server implementation for agentic-robotics, defining MCP request/response types, tool registry, and request dispatch for initialize, list_tools, and call_tool.

nodes.name: McpServer
nodes.summary: Core MCP server holding a tool registry and ServerInfo; dispatches initialize, list_tools, and call_tool JSON-RPC methods to registered handlers.

nodes.name: McpTool
nodes.summary: MCP tool descriptor pairing a tool name, description, and JSON input_schema for client introspection.

nodes.name: McpRequest
nodes.summary: Incoming MCP request envelope carrying jsonrpc version, id, method, and params payload.

nodes.name: McpResponse
nodes.summary: MCP response envelope wrapping either a successful result or an McpError back to the caller.

nodes.name: handle_request
nodes.summary: Top-level MCP request router dispatching to initialize, list_tools, or call_tool based on the method field.

nodes.name: handle_call_tool
nodes.summary: Validates incoming tools/call payloads, locates the registered handler, and forwards parameters returning structured ToolResult output.

nodes.name: handle_list_tools
nodes.summary: Returns the catalog of registered MCP tools with their schemas in response to a tools/list request.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the agentic-robotics-node crate that builds the NAPI-RS Node.js bindings for the robotics runtime.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: NPM manifest declaring NAPI-RS native bindings, build scripts, and metadata for publishing agentic-robotics as a Node.js package.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Real-time runtime documentation covering priority scheduling, deadline enforcement, and latency monitoring across 26 sections with usage examples.

nodes.name: latency.rs
nodes.summary: Latency tracking utilities built on a histogram, exposing tracker, stats (min/max/mean/p50/p90/p99/p999), and RAII measurement guard for real-time loops.

nodes.name: LatencyTracker
nodes.summary: Named latency tracker backed by a histogram with record, stats snapshot, reset, and scoped measure helpers.

nodes.name: LatencyStats
nodes.summary: Snapshot of latency statistics including count, min, max, mean, and p50/p90/p99/p999 percentiles with a Display impl.

nodes.name: LatencyMeasurement
nodes.summary: RAII guard that automatically records elapsed time into a LatencyTracker when dropped.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the ruvector-robotics crate declaring features, dependencies, benches, and lint configurations for the robotics integration layer.

nodes.name: behavior_tree.rs
nodes.summary: Runnable example constructing a small behavior tree and ticking it to demonstrate the cognitive::behavior_tree API.

nodes.name: spatial_indexing.rs
nodes.summary: Runnable example showing point-cloud ingestion and nearest-neighbor search via the ruvector-robotics bridge layer.

nodes.name: converters.rs
nodes.summary: Conversion helpers translating robotics primitives (point clouds, robot state, poses, occupancy grids, scene graphs) into RuVector-friendly vector representations.

nodes.name: point_cloud_to_vectors_with_intensity
nodes.summary: Converts a PointCloud with per-point intensities into 4D vectors (x,y,z,intensity) for RuVector indexing.

nodes.name: vectors_to_point_cloud
nodes.summary: Reverse conversion building a PointCloud from a slice of 3D or 4D RuVector vectors at a given timestamp.

nodes.name: scene_graph_to_adjacency
nodes.summary: Flattens a SceneGraph into an adjacency representation suitable for graph-based reasoning over scene objects.

nodes.name: ConversionError
nodes.summary: Error enum for bridge conversion failures distinguishing length mismatch from empty input cases.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Bridge module defining core robotics primitives (Point3D, Quaternion, Pose, PointCloud, RobotState, SensorFrame, OccupancyGrid, SceneGraph, Trajectory) shared across crates.

nodes.name: Point3D
nodes.summary: 3D point primitive with distance, array, and vector conversion helpers used throughout robotics geometry.

nodes.name: Quaternion
nodes.summary: Quaternion orientation primitive providing identity, default, and explicit constructors.

nodes.name: Pose
nodes.summary: Pose primitive combining position, orientation quaternion, and a frame_id for coordinate-frame-aware geometry.

nodes.name: PointCloud
nodes.summary: Point cloud container with points, optional intensities and normals, timestamp, and frame_id metadata.

nodes.name: RobotState
nodes.summary: Robot kinematic state with position, velocity, acceleration, and microsecond timestamp.

nodes.name: OccupancyGrid
nodes.summary: 2D occupancy grid with width, height, resolution, origin, and indexed get/set accessors for mapping and planning.

nodes.name: SceneGraph
nodes.summary: Scene graph aggregating SceneObject vertices and SceneEdge relations with a timestamp.

nodes.name: SensorFrame
nodes.summary: Composite sensor frame bundling a PointCloud, RobotState, Pose, and timestamp for synchronized perception updates.

nodes.name: behavior_tree.rs
nodes.summary: Cognitive behavior-tree engine with Action/Condition/Sequence/Selector/Decorator/Parallel nodes, decorator semantics, and a tickable BehaviorTree with blackboard context.

nodes.name: BehaviorTree
nodes.summary: Tickable behavior tree pairing a root node with a BehaviorContext exposing conditions, action results, and a blackboard.

nodes.name: BehaviorNode
nodes.summary: Tagged enum of behavior tree node variants: Action, Condition, Sequence, Selector, Decorator, and Parallel.

nodes.name: BehaviorContext
nodes.summary: Per-tick context holding a blackboard, tick counter, condition map, and action results consumed during evaluation.

nodes.name: eval
nodes.summary: Recursive evaluator dispatching each BehaviorNode variant against the current BehaviorContext to produce a status.

nodes.name: eval_decorator
nodes.summary: Applies decorator semantics (Inverter, Repeat, UntilFail, Timeout) by recursively evaluating the wrapped child node.

nodes.name: memory_system.rs
nodes.summary: Tri-layer agent memory system providing bounded WorkingMemory, similarity-based EpisodicMemory, and embedding-backed SemanticMemory with cosine retrieval.

nodes.name: WorkingMemory
nodes.summary: Bounded working memory keyed by string with importance-aware eviction and access-count tracking.

nodes.name: EpisodicMemory
nodes.summary: Episodic memory storing (percepts, actions, reward) episodes with k-NN recall by similarity to a query.

nodes.name: SemanticMemory
nodes.summary: Named-concept embedding store with retrieve-by-name and find_similar k-NN search via dot-product similarity.

nodes.name: swarm_intelligence.rs
nodes.summary: Swarm coordinator managing multi-robot task assignment, formation control (Line, Circle, Grid, Custom), and consensus voting across registered robots.

nodes.name: SwarmCoordinator
nodes.summary: Central swarm coordinator registering robot capabilities and orchestrating task assignment, formation computation, and consensus proposals.

nodes.name: assign_tasks
nodes.summary: Capability-aware task allocator pairing SwarmTasks with registered robots based on priority and required capabilities.

nodes.name: compute_formation
nodes.summary: Computes target positions for each robot to assemble Line, Circle, Grid, or Custom formations around a center point.

nodes.name: propose_consensus
nodes.summary: Runs a vote on a proposal across the swarm, accepting it when the configured consensus threshold is reached.

nodes.name: SwarmConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration for SwarmCoordinator specifying max_robots, communication_range, and consensus_threshold.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Crate root for ruvector-robotics aggregating bridge, cognitive, perception, planning, mcp, rvf, and domain_expansion submodules.
nodes.languageNotes: Rust crate root using pub mod declarations to expose all subsystems.

nodes.name: clustering.rs
nodes.summary: Spatial hashing + union-find point-cloud clustering producing labeled groups by occupied-cell adjacency for downstream obstacle detection.

nodes.name: cluster_point_cloud
nodes.summary: Clusters a PointCloud by hashing points into spatial cells of cell_size and union-finding adjacent occupied cells.

nodes.name: obstacle_detector.rs
nodes.summary: Obstacle detector consuming clustered point clouds, fitting bounding extents, and classifying obstacles as Static, Dynamic, or Unknown.

nodes.name: ObstacleDetector
nodes.summary: Configurable obstacle detector exposing detect, cluster_to_obstacle, and classify_obstacles pipelines.

nodes.name: DetectedObstacle
nodes.summary: Raw detected obstacle with center, extent, point_count, and min_distance to the robot.

nodes.name: ClassifiedObstacle
nodes.summary: Detected obstacle augmented with an ObstacleClass label and a confidence score.

nodes.name: detect
nodes.summary: Runs clustering on a PointCloud and converts each cluster into a DetectedObstacle relative to the robot position.

nodes.name: cluster_to_obstacle
nodes.summary: Aggregates a cluster of points into a DetectedObstacle by computing bounding extent and minimum distance to the robot.

nodes.name: planning.rs
nodes.summary: Motion planning module exposing A* search on OccupancyGrids plus a reactive potential-field velocity controller and path-to-waypoint utilities.

nodes.name: astar
nodes.summary: A* shortest-path search over an OccupancyGrid returning a GridPath of cells or a PlanningError when no path exists.

nodes.name: potential_field
nodes.summary: Reactive potential-field controller summing attractive force toward goal and repulsive forces from obstacles to produce a VelocityCommand.

nodes.name: path_to_waypoints
nodes.summary: Converts a discrete A* GridPath into world-space waypoints given grid resolution and origin.

nodes.name: GridPath
nodes.summary: Planner output containing the ordered grid cells of a path along with its total traversal cost.

nodes.name: PlanningError
nodes.summary: Error enum distinguishing InvalidStart, InvalidGoal, and NoPath outcomes from planning calls.

nodes.name: PotentialFieldConfig
nodes.summary: Tunable potential-field gains and limits: attractive_gain, repulsive_gain, obstacle_influence, and max_speed.

nodes.name: robotics_integration.rs
nodes.summary: Integration test suite (20 tests) exercising perception, cognition (memory/behavior-tree/swarm), MCP tool calls, Gaussian splatting, A*/potential-field planning, sensor fusion, and concurrent spatial search.

layers.name: Agentic Robotics Core Middleware
layers.description: Zenoh/RustDDS pub-sub middleware: message types, serialization, publisher/subscriber, service abstractions, and shared error/middleware primitives for the agentic-robotics-core crate.

layers.name: Agentic Robotics Real-Time Scheduler
layers.description: Real-time priority scheduling and ROS3-style executor: agentic-robotics-rt scheduler, executor, and latency-tracking primitives.

layers.name: Agentic Robotics Peripheral Surfaces
layers.description: Outward-facing integration surfaces for the agentic-robotics stack: Embassy-based embedded runtime (no_std), MCP server with stdio transport, and NAPI-RS Node.js bindings (lib + build.rs).

layers.name: RuVector Robotics Bridge
layers.description: Bridge layer wiring robotics data to RuVector vector store: config, indexing, search, converters, Gaussian-splat ingestion, pipeline orchestration, and the crate root (lib/planning/rvf/domain_expansion).

layers.name: RuVector Robotics Perception
layers.description: Perception subsystem: scene graph, sensor fusion, clustering, obstacle detection, and perception-pipeline configuration on top of RuVector.

layers.name: RuVector Robotics Cognition
layers.description: Cognitive core implementing perceive-think-act-learn: decision engine, world model, behavior tree, memory system, skill learning, and swarm intelligence.

layers.name: RuVector Robotics MCP Tools
layers.description: MCP tool registry and executor that exposes ruvector-robotics capabilities as callable Model Context Protocol tools.

layers.name: Slice 8 Tests, Benches, and Examples
layers.description: Integration tests, criterion benchmarks (pub-sub latency, message serialization, executor performance, message passing, robotics benchmarks), and runnable examples (cognitive_loop, swarm_coordination, obstacle_detection, behavior_tree, spatial_indexing) for the robotics slice.

layers.name: Slice 8 Documentation and Crate Config
layers.description: Cargo.toml manifests, package.json, and per-crate README documentation for the seven robotics crates.

tour.title: Slice 8: Robotics Stack Orientation
tour.description: Slice 8 layers a full robotics stack on top of RuVector: a Zenoh/RustDDS publish-subscribe middleware (agentic-robotics-*) with real-time scheduling, an embedded variant, MCP and NAPI surfaces, plus a ruvector-robotics crate that wires perception, cognition, motion planning, and Gaussian-splat rendering into the vector store. Start with the ruvector-robotics README to see the bridge/perception/cognitive/mcp module split and the sub-100us perception latency targets that justify the architecture, then read the agentic-robotics-core README for the ROS2-compatible pub/sub framing the slice depends on.

tour.title: Slice 8: Pub-Sub Middleware Core
tour.description: agentic-robotics-core is the messaging substrate. Its lib.rs re-exports publisher/subscriber/message/middleware/serialization/service and exposes an init() bootstrap, while middleware.rs wraps Zenoh as the primary pub/sub session opener. Everything else in slice 8 either produces messages onto this bus or consumes them — perception, cognition, and the MCP transport all sit downstream of these abstractions.
tour.languageLesson: Rust crate roots use `pub use` re-exports to flatten a multi-module crate into a tidy public surface; `mod x; pub use x::*;` lets consumers write `agentic_robotics_core::Publisher` instead of `agentic_robotics_core::publisher::Publisher`.

tour.title: Slice 8: Wire Format & Message Types
tour.description: The bus speaks ROS2-compatible types. message.rs declares the Message trait plus payload structs RobotState, PointCloud, Pose, and Point3D — the highest fan-in nodes in the slice because every publisher, subscriber, perception module, and benchmark imports them. serialization.rs offers a Serializer enum dispatching across CDR (the ROS2 wire format), rkyv zero-copy, and JSON, while error.rs centralizes the crate's thiserror-based Error enum.
tour.languageLesson: thiserror derives std::error::Error on an enum, letting each variant carry a #[error("...")] format string. Combined with `?`, this gives ergonomic error propagation while preserving variant-specific context — the idiomatic alternative to anyhow inside library crates.

tour.title: Slice 8: Publisher, Subscriber & Services
tour.description: Building on the Message and Serializer types from Step 3, publisher.rs is a typed Publisher that serializes onto a topic and tracks per-publisher byte/message counters; subscriber.rs offers blocking, non-blocking, and async receive with clone-for-fan-out semantics; service.rs adds the Queryable/Service request-response pair plus ServiceStats. Together these three modules define the entire user-visible API of the messaging core.

tour.title: Slice 8: Real-Time Scheduler & Executor
tour.description: Robotics control loops need deadline guarantees the default tokio runtime can't make. agentic-robotics-rt provides RTPriority (Background through Critical), a PriorityScheduler implementing a deadline-aware min-heap over ScheduledTasks, and the ROS3Executor that splits work across separate high/low-priority tokio runtimes plus a blocking pool. latency.rs adds a histogram-based tracker exposing p50/p90/p99/p999 stats and an RAII guard you drop at the end of a control loop iteration to record its duration.
tour.languageLesson: RAII measurement guards exploit Rust's Drop trait: the guard captures a start Instant on construction and records elapsed time in its destructor, so a single `let _g = tracker.measure();` at the top of a scope automatically reports the scope's wall time without explicit stop() calls — and is exception-safe because Drop still runs on panic.

tour.title: Slice 8: Embassy Embedded Variant
tour.description: agentic-robotics-embedded is the no_std cousin that lets the same priority and timing primitives run on microcontrollers under RTIC or Embassy. lib.rs declares EmbeddedPriority levels and an EmbeddedConfig with tick-rate and stack-size knobs sized for sub-50KB targets, demonstrating that slice 8 was designed for both server-class and edge deployments from the same source.
tour.languageLesson: Rust's no_std feature flag opts a crate out of the standard library so it compiles for bare-metal microcontrollers. The crate keeps `core` (intrinsics, primitives) and optionally `alloc` (heap types via a custom allocator), enabling identical algorithmic code to target both servers and MCUs by flipping a Cargo feature.

tour.title: Slice 8: NAPI Node.js Bindings
tour.description: agentic-robotics-node lifts the Rust pub/sub core into JavaScript. lib.rs uses NAPI-RS to expose AgenticNode/AgenticPublisher/AgenticSubscriber to Node.js, marshalling JSON between V8 and the Rust bus with async publish, try_recv, and recv. build.rs is currently a no-op placeholder while package.json declares the @napi-rs build pipeline and metadata so Node developers can `npm install` the prebuilt binary.
tour.languageLesson: NAPI-RS generates V8/Node ABI shims from #[napi] attributes, producing a .node prebuilt per target triple. JSON-serialised bridging at the call boundary is the simplest interop pattern: V8 owns the JS objects, Rust owns the typed structs, and serde reconciles them on every call.

tour.title: Slice 8: Robotics MCP Server & Transport
tour.description: agentic-robotics-mcp exposes the pub-sub stack to LLM agents via the Model Context Protocol. lib.rs defines MCP request/response types, a tool registry, and dispatch for initialize, list_tools, and call_tool; server.rs is a fluent ServerBuilder with helpers (tool registration, text_response, error_response) that translate robotics primitives into spec-compliant JSON; transport.rs implements StdioTransport, a line-delimited JSON channel over stdin/stdout — the same wire shape Claude Code already uses to talk to MCP servers.

tour.title: Slice 8: Vector Bridge & Spatial Index
tour.description: ruvector-robotics begins where agentic-robotics-core ends: the bridge folder is where sensor data crosses into the vector store. bridge/mod.rs declares the shared primitives (Point3D, Quaternion, Pose, PointCloud, RobotState, SensorFrame, OccupancyGrid, SceneGraph, Trajectory) — the highest fan-in node in the slice. converters.rs marshals those primitives into RuVector vectors, indexing.rs builds a SpatialIndex with KNN and radius search across Euclidean/Manhattan/cosine metrics, search.rs defines the Neighbor/SearchResult/ObstacleAlert result types, and gaussian.rs packs point clouds into compact Gaussian splats for 3D viewer rendering.

tour.title: Slice 8: Perception Pipeline
tour.description: Perception turns raw frames into structured scene understanding. perception/mod.rs declares PerceptionPipeline orchestrating obstacle detection, scene graph construction, trajectory prediction, attention focusing, and anomaly detection. scene_graph.rs computes proximity-based scene graphs with semantic near/far edges and timestamp-preserving cross-frame merges; clustering.rs uses spatial hashing plus union-find to label point clouds; obstacle_detector.rs fits bounding extents and classifies obstacles as Static/Dynamic/Unknown; sensor_fusion.rs voxel-downsamples multi-sensor clouds; config.rs is the serde-roundtripped configuration glue.

tour.title: Slice 8: Cognition (Perceive-Think-Act-Learn)
tour.description: Above perception sits cognition. cognitive_core.rs implements the perceive-think-act-learn loop with attention-thresholded percept admission, confidence-ranked decision selection, emergency-mode preemption, and reward-driven threshold adaptation. world_model.rs tracks objects with kinematic prediction over a 2D occupancy grid; decision_engine.rs is a utility-based scorer over ActionOption candidates; behavior_tree.rs ticks Action/Condition/Sequence/Selector/Decorator/Parallel nodes against a blackboard; memory_system.rs gives a tri-layer working/episodic/semantic memory with cosine retrieval; skill_learning.rs grows a SkillLibrary from demonstrations; swarm_intelligence.rs adds multi-robot task assignment, formation control, and consensus voting.

tour.title: Slice 8: Planning, RVF Packing & Domain Expansion
tour.description: Three connective modules sit at the crate's edge. planning.rs implements A* search over the OccupancyGrid produced by the world model in Step 11 plus a reactive potential-field velocity controller and waypoint utilities. rvf.rs is the RuVector File packing layer that adapts robotics primitives (point clouds, scene objects, trajectories, Gaussian splats, obstacles) into the vector store with strict dimension enforcement and kNN passthrough. domain_expansion.rs defines a RoboticsDomain task generator covering clustering, avoidance, scene graph, skill sequencing, and swarm formation — used by evaluation harnesses to score robotics behaviour.

tour.title: Slice 8: Robotics MCP Tools
tour.description: The robotics MCP module is the agent-facing capstone of ruvector-robotics. mcp/mod.rs declares tool parameters, schemas, the RoboticsToolRegistry, and register_defaults that populates ~30 robotics tools (perceive, plan, swarm, learn) for AI-assistant orchestration. mcp/executor.rs is the dispatcher: it routes LLM JSON requests to PerceptionPipeline methods like detect_obstacles, build_scene_graph, predict_trajectory, focus_attention, detect_anomalies, spatial_search, and insert_points, recording per-call latency for the same histograms introduced in Step 5.

tour.title: Slice 8: Examples, Benchmarks & Integration
tour.description: Close the tour with the runnable artefacts that exercise everything above end-to-end. examples/cognitive_loop.rs drives the full perceive-think-act-learn cycle on synthetic input; swarm_coordination.rs registers robots with a SwarmCoordinator, assigns tasks, and runs consensus; obstacle_detection.rs, behavior_tree.rs, and spatial_indexing.rs are minimal demos for the named subsystems. tests/robotics_integration.rs is a 20-test suite covering perception, memory, behaviour trees, swarm, MCP tool calls, Gaussian splatting, A* and potential-field planning, sensor fusion, and concurrent spatial search. agentic-robotics-benchmarks/benches/pubsub_latency.rs measures Zenoh/RustDDS publish latency, throughput, end-to-end latency, CDR-vs-JSON cost, and concurrent-publisher scaling on the messaging core from Steps 2-4.
